fields.label: Account Name
fields.description: Human-readable label for this deployment's EasyEcom account.

fields.label: Enabled
fields.description: Account-wide kill-switch. When unchecked, no syncs of any kind run for any location.

fields.label: Connection Status
fields.description: Live indicator. Computed every 60s from connection health.

fields.label: API Endpoint
fields.description: EasyEcom base URL. Production: https://api.easyecom.io. Sandbox URL differs.

fields.label: X-API-Key
fields.description: Account-level API key. Generated only from the client's Primary Seller Account; regenerating it invalidates the previous key instantly.

fields.label: Account Email
fields.description: EasyEcom account email (must be a user with multi-location access in the primary account). Treated as a credential (§3.7.1).

fields.label: Rate Limit Tier
fields.description: Tier EasyEcom has assigned to this api_key. No preset default — set to whatever EE has actually assigned. Drives throttle and daily quota (§3.10). A newly generated key sits in Default tier until EE upgrades it; "tier still Default at go-live" is a blocking onboarding condition.

fields.label: Default Location
fields.description: Used for location-agnostic operations (typically the primary location for master ops).

fields.label: Test Connection
fields.description: Triggers a /access/token call against the default_location_key and reports result inline.

fields.label: Enable Sync Window
fields.description: If unchecked, syncs run 24x7.

fields.label: Daily End
fields.description: May cross midnight.

fields.label: Pause Until
fields.description: Manual pause — overrides the window.

fields.label: Window Exemptions
fields.description: Critical jobs that bypass the window — typically webhook-triggered.

fields.label: Max Throughput (req/s)
fields.description: Outbound rate cap per location_key. The integration clamps to the rate_limit_tier ceiling regardless.

fields.label: Max Concurrent Workers
fields.description: Account-wide. Per-Company sub-limits in §6.3.7.

fields.label: Block SO on Push Error
fields.description: If SO Push Mode is Sync: reject on_submit when EE errors. Per-Marketplace choice (§2.4).

fields.label: Webhooks Enabled
fields.description: If unchecked, all inbound webhooks return 503; integration operates in poll-only mode.

fields.label: Webhook Token
fields.description: Shared bearer token issued to EasyEcom. Required if webhooks are enabled.

fields.label: Webhook Endpoint URL
fields.description: The URL to register in EasyEcom. The receiver resolves the originating location to its Company on receipt.

fields.label: Allowed IPs (CIDR, one per line)
fields.description: Optional CIDR allowlist. Requests from outside the list are rejected.

fields.label: Default Rejected Warehouse
fields.description: Where rejected_qty from GRN lines posts. Resolved per receiving Company.

fields.label: Item Master Mode
fields.description: Controls the §8d Item / Product master flow. Onboarding (default) is bidirectional and FDE-supervised; ERPNext-Mastered is the steady state where ERPNext owns the catalogue and EE-side changes show up as Drift flags rather than auto-overwriting. The flip is explicit and one-way (until manually reverted).

fields.label: Item Master Mode
fields.description: §8.1.1. `onboarding` = bidirectional pull + push, supervised; `erpnext_mastered` = ERPNext is source of truth, pull becomes drift-detection only. Read-only on the form — the FDE flips via the Flip action below (with confirmation).

fields.label: Flipped to ERPNext-Mastered At
fields.description: Audit timestamp of when the FDE flipped to ERPNext-mastered. Empty during onboarding.

fields.label: Flip → ERPNext-Mastered
fields.description: One-way flip: marks onboarding complete and switches the §8d pull from accept-and-create to drift-detection-only. The FDE confirms in a dialog before the flip lands. Reverse flips (back to onboarding) require manual intervention — design intent is one flip per client lifecycle.

fields.label: Customer Master Mode
fields.description: Controls the §8.2 Customer master flow. Mirrors Item Master Mode exactly: Onboarding (default) is bidirectional and FDE-supervised; ERPNext-Mastered is the steady state where ERPNext owns the customer master and EE-side changes show up as Drift flags rather than auto-overwriting. The flip is explicit and one-way (until manually reverted). Independent of the Item flip — accounts can flip Customer master while still in Item onboarding (or vice versa).

fields.label: Customer Master Mode
fields.description: §8.2. `onboarding` = bidirectional pull + push, supervised; `erpnext_mastered` = ERPNext is source of truth, pull becomes drift-detection only. Read-only on the form — the FDE flips via the Flip action below (with confirmation).

fields.label: Customer Master Flipped to ERPNext-Mastered At
fields.description: Audit timestamp of when the FDE flipped Customer master to ERPNext-mastered. Empty during onboarding.

fields.label: Flip Customers → ERPNext-Mastered
fields.description: One-way flip for Customer master: marks onboarding complete and switches the §8.2 pull from accept-and-create to drift-detection-only. The FDE confirms in a dialog before the flip lands. Reverse flips require manual intervention.

fields.label: Refresh States/Countries
fields.description: §8e Stage 2 — discover-and-cache /getCountries + /getStates. Pure foundational reference data (no entity mapping, no Sync Records, mode-irrelevant). The 8.2 push needs the int stateId for CreateCustomer's billingStateId/dispatchStateId; the pull uses the cache to soft-validate pincode-vs-state and to translate names. Idempotent — re-runs refresh in-place; never deletes legacy rows.

fields.label: Discover Customers
fields.description: §8e Stage 3 — pull wholesale customers from /Wholesale/v2/UserManagement?type=b2b. Creates one Customer + Billing Address + Shipping Address + Customer Map row per EE c_id. Matching: map row exists → reuse; else create new (no natural-key auto-match — gstNum and companyname are dirty/duplicated in real EE data). India Compliance is authoritative on tax-relevant validation: any GST place-of-supply throw (invalid gstin, gstin-state mismatch, pincode-state mismatch) → whole Customer Flagged-Not-Created (held). Run Refresh States/Countries first if the cache is empty.

fields.label: Auto-push Customers to EasyEcom on save
fields.description: §8e Stage 4 — when ON: every ERPNext Customer create/edit enqueues a /Wholesale/CreateCustomer or /UpdateCustomer push. Defaults OFF for safety — accidental enable on a fresh deployment would push every existing Customer with customer_type=Company. Turn ON only when you've configured credentials and want steady-state push automation. The push is queued (non-blocking on save) and obeys the same flagging logic as the batch sweep — unresolvable state / missing email / missing mandatory → flag-not-pushed (map row), not a broken EE payload. The pull-vs-push ping-pong guard prevents loops (a Customer that was just pulled by Discover Customers doesn't immediately re-push).

fields.label: Push All Pending Customers
fields.description: §8e Stage 4 — batch sweep: enqueue a /Wholesale/CreateCustomer push for every candidate Customer (customer_type=Company; no EasyEcom Customer Map row yet; has email_id; is enabled). Returns immediately with the count enqueued; per-Customer progress lands in Sync Records / Queue Job rows. Non-candidates (already on EE, missing email, individual-type) are skipped at the candidate-query level.

fields.label: Supplier Master Mode
fields.description: Controls the §8.3 Supplier master flow. Mirrors Customer Master Mode exactly: Onboarding (default) is bidirectional and FDE-supervised; ERPNext-Mastered is the steady state where ERPNext owns the supplier master and EE-side changes show up as Drift flags rather than auto-overwriting. The flip is explicit and one-way (until manually reverted). INDEPENDENT of the Item/Customer flips — accounts can flip Supplier master while still in Customer onboarding (or vice versa).

fields.label: Supplier Master Mode
fields.description: §8.3. `onboarding` = bidirectional pull + push, supervised; `erpnext_mastered` = ERPNext is source of truth, pull becomes drift-detection only. Read-only on the form — the FDE flips via the Flip action below (with confirmation).

fields.label: Supplier Master Flipped to ERPNext-Mastered At
fields.description: Audit timestamp of when the FDE flipped Supplier master to ERPNext-mastered. Empty during onboarding.

fields.label: Flip Suppliers → ERPNext-Mastered
fields.description: One-way flip for Supplier master: marks onboarding complete and switches the §8.3 pull from accept-and-create to drift-detection-only. The FDE confirms in a dialog before the flip lands. Reverse flips require manual intervention.

fields.label: Discover Suppliers (§8f Pull)
fields.description: Trigger the §8f pull — cursor-walks /wms/V2/getVendors, creates Suppliers + Billing/Dispatch Addresses + Supplier Map rows, applies country-aware GST gating (Indian → IC validates GSTIN+PAN, bad → FNC; foreign → gst_category=Overseas), and propagates active:0 → Supplier.disabled. Resumable via supplier_pull_cursor. Post-flip (supplier_master_mode=erpnext_mastered), the same button runs in drift-detection mode (no mutations; EE-side changes land as Drift map rows).

fields.label: Supplier Pull Resume Cursor
fields.description: EE getVendors nextUrl. Persisted page-by-page so an interrupted walk resumes from the last completed page rather than restarting. Cleared on full-pull start; advanced after each page commits. Mirrors §8d's item_pull_cursor pattern.

fields.label: Last Successful Supplier Pull (updated_after high-water)
fields.description: High-water mark for incremental deltas. The next scheduled pull passes this as the getVendors updated_after parameter (vendors HAVE delta-pull support, unlike customers' flat-pull). Set only on a clean full-walk (no failed pages); a partial run leaves the previous value untouched so the next run still covers the gap. Stage 6 wires the cron; Stage 3 just stores the watermark.

fields.label: Total Suppliers Seen (last successful pull)
fields.description: Aggregate count from the last full successful pull — used by the FDE to monitor that the supplier base size is sane.

fields.label: Auto-push Suppliers to EasyEcom on save
fields.description: §8f Stage 4 — when ON: every ERPNext Supplier create/edit enqueues a /wms/CreateVendor or /wms/UpdateVendor push. Defaults OFF for safety. Turn ON only when you've configured credentials and want steady-state push automation. The push is queued (non-blocking on save) and obeys the same flagging logic as the batch sweep — unresolvable country / missing mandatory field / IC throws on Indian GSTIN → flag-not-pushed (Supplier Map row), not a broken EE payload. The pull-vs-push ping-pong guard prevents loops (a Supplier that was just pulled by Discover Suppliers doesn't immediately re-push).

fields.label: Push All Pending Suppliers
fields.description: §8f Stage 4 — batch sweep: enqueue a /wms/CreateVendor push for every candidate Supplier (supplier_type=Company; no EasyEcom Supplier Map row yet; disabled=0). Returns immediately with the count enqueued; per-Supplier progress lands in Sync Records / Queue Job rows. Non-candidates (already on EE, individual-type, disabled) are skipped at the candidate-query level.

fields.label: Item Pull (§8d)
fields.description: Account-wide §8d pull state: the resume cursor for the GetProductMaster walk, the high-water mark for incremental (updated_after) deltas, and the default UOM used when EE's accounting_unit is dirty/missing (the Created-Flagged trigger surfaces this).

fields.label: Default UOM
fields.description: Substituted into stock_uom when EE's accounting_unit is missing or doesn't match an ERPNext UOM (real values seen: '111', '333', '11', 'PCS', 'NA'). The item is created with this UOM and flagged Created-Flagged so the FDE can correct.

fields.label: Resume Cursor
fields.description: EE GetProductMaster nextUrl. Persisted page-by-page so an interrupted walk resumes from the last completed page rather than restarting. Cleared on full-pull start; advanced after each page commits.

fields.label: Last Successful Pull (updated_after high-water)
fields.description: High-water mark for incremental deltas. The next scheduled pull passes this as the GetProductMaster updated_after parameter. Set only on a clean full-walk (no failed pages); a partial run leaves the previous value untouched so the next run still covers the gap.

fields.label: Total Products Seen (last successful pull)
fields.description: From GetProductMastersCount at the start of the last full pull — used by the FDE to monitor whether the catalogue size is sane.

fields.label: Discover Products (§8d Pull)
fields.description: Trigger the §8d pull — cursor-walks GetProductMaster, creates/refreshes Items + Product Bundles, stamps multi-Company tax, surfaces flagged rows for the FDE. Post-flip (item_master_mode=erpnext_mastered), this same button runs in drift-detection mode (no mutations; EE-side changes land as Drift map rows).

fields.label: Item Push (§8d)
fields.description: ERPNext → EasyEcom push: the onboarding batch sweep + the auto-push hook that fires on every Item save when enabled. In steady state (after the flip), push is the authoritative direction for item changes.

fields.label: Auto-push Items to EasyEcom on save
fields.description: When ON: every ERPNext Item create/edit (and every Product Bundle save) enqueues a §8d push to EasyEcom. Defaults OFF for safety — accidental enable on a fresh deployment would push every existing Item. Turn ON only when you've configured credentials and want steady-state push automation. The push is queued (non-blocking on save) and obeys the same flagging logic as the batch sweep — missing-mandatory fields → FNC map row, not a broken EE payload.

fields.label: Push All Pending Items (§8d Onboarding Sweep)
fields.description: Trigger the §8d batch push for every Item not yet pushed: is_stock_item=1, not disabled, has gst_hsn_code, NO existing map with ee_product_id. Bundle wrappers dispatch to combo push. Resumable — re-running picks up only still-pending items. Used for one-shot onboarding; steady-state changes flow via the auto-push hook.

fields.label: Steady-State Controls (Go Live)
fields.description: Controlled transition between onboarding mode (auto-push OFF; FDE triggers each pull/push manually) and steady state (auto-push ON; every ERPNext-side doc save propagates to EE). Use Go Live ONCE per client after onboarding reconciliation is complete; use Pause as an emergency kill-switch. Both actions record an audit Comment on this Account doc.

fields.label: Go Live — Enable Auto-Push
fields.description: Steady-state transition. Flips auto_push_on_save (Items), auto_push_customers_on_save, auto_push_suppliers_on_save all to ON in one action — FDE picks which entities in the confirm dialog. Warns (but doesn't block) when an entity is still in onboarding master-mode. Audit-logged via Comment with timestamp + user + which entities.

fields.label: Pause All Auto-Push (Kill-Switch)
fields.description: Emergency disable — sets all three auto-push toggles to OFF in one transaction. Use during incidents (EE rate-limit storm, bad ERPNext config polluting EE, etc.) to stop the on_update hooks from firing. Idempotent. The JS handler prompts for a reason; the reason lands in the audit Comment for post-incident review. Re-enable via Go Live.
